title: sesamoides pygmaea subsp. prostratus
intro: Sesamoides pygmaea subsp. prostratus, also known as prostrate sesame, is a low-growing, mat-forming shrub native to the alpine regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a natural antioxidant. Sesamoides pygmaea subsp. prostratus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other minor health conditions.
